title: 12-Inch Cast Iron Skillet Cornbread
ingredients: ½ stick butter (optional and not pictured)
steps: Preheat oven to 400 degrees. In a small bowl, combine cornmeal and milk. Let it rest for at least 5 minutes.

Grease a 12-inch cast iron pan. (If you do not have a 12-inch pan, this recipe works well with a 13 x 9 inch baking pan. A 12-inch pan is about 113 square inches, while a 13 x 9 inch pan is 117 square inches.)
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Mix in the cornmeal mixture, eggs, and oil until smooth.

This is an optional step that I just started using recently. It isn't necessary, but it does produce a much richer tasting cornbread. While you are whisking the ingredients above, put ½ stick of butter in the preheated cast iron pan in the oven to melt.
Pour batter into the prepared pan.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a knife inserted into the center of the cornbread comes out clean.
